decision: Field explanation cache uses DB (PostgreSQL via Prisma) rather than Redis or in-memory
why: No Redis in the stack; DB cache is persistent across deploys and serverless function restarts (Vercel), which is important for a cost-reduction cache. Acceptable latency since Prisma batch lookups are fast and cache misses still fall through to Claude anyway.

decision: Cache key is normalized_label:type (e.g. first_name:text) rather than including form context
why: Field explanations for common fields like First Name or Email are universal — they don't depend on which form they appear in. Global keying maximises cross-form reuse and is the main cost-saving mechanism.

decision: Cache is applied as a post-pass overlay over the Claude response rather than skipping Claude for cached fields
why: Claude still provides the form title, description, field IDs, and estimatedMinutes which are form-specific. Skipping Claude entirely would require a two-phase design (first extract structure, then enrich). The overlay approach is simpler and already reduces cost once cached data is used — future work can skip Claude for fully-cached forms.

decision: Store uploaded file bytes in DB (Prisma Bytes / PostgreSQL BYTEA) rather than Vercel Blob or S3 for MVP
why: No external storage service needed; keeps the stack simple for MVP. The 10MB file size limit is already enforced at upload, so DB row sizes remain bounded. Blob storage migration is straightforward when needed — the fileKey column already exists as a placeholder.
